original: 150 million year old fossil egg found in Utah with a fossilized dinosaur embryo inside, the oldest dinosaur egg yet found in the Northern Hemisphere
Scientists identify the first animal that doesn't need oxygen to breathe: a tiny parasite living in salmon tissue, reported in the journal PNAS
A 4.4 billion-year-old crystal is discovered as the oldest known fragment of Earth's crust
US ice hockey team clinches gold medal with 4-2 win over Finland at the Lake Placid Winter Olympics; comes after 4-3 "Miracle on Ice" victory against hot favorite Soviet Union
Wayne Gretzkyscores NHL-record 78th goal of season en route to 92
Ester Ledecká of the Czech Republic becomes first female to win Olympic gold medals in 2 different sports at a single Winter Games (Pyeongchang); skiing super-G and snowboarding parallel giant slalom
Final launch of Space Shuttle Discovery (OV-103)
Discovery of 1st pulsar announced (CP 1919) byJocelyn Bell Burnelland Antony Hewish
Mariner 6 launched for Mars flyby to study planet's atmosphere
Dutch speed skater Atje Keulen-Deelstra becomes Women's Allround World Champion at Heerenveen, Netherlands, her third consecutive title and fourth in 5 years
Malcolm Campbellsets world land speed record speed of 253.96 mph driving his famous Blue Bird car at Daytona Beach, Florida
Sachin Tendulkarscores the first double century in One Day International cricket
Joe Sakicand Jerome Iginla each score twice as Canada defeats the United States, 5-2 to win the men's ice hockey Olympic gold medal at Salt Lake City
THe Beatles begin filming their second feature fim "Help!" on Providence Island in the Bahamas
Jacques de Vaucanson, French inventor of the automaton (Flute Player), born in Grenoble, France (d. 1782)[1]
South Africa announces it is constructing the largest modern-day blimp
De Lesseps' Company begins work on Panama Canal
Memorial service for NBA star Kobe Byrant held at the Staples Center in Los Angeles
Britain'sPrince Charlesannounces engagement to LadyDiana Spencer
Elton Johnis knighted byQueen Elizabeth IIat Buckingham Palace in London
"Flying Scotsman" locomotive of the London and North Eastern Railway (LNER), built at Doncaster Railway Works, goes into service
Chris Buck, American animator and film director (Tarzan, Surf's Up, Frozen), born in Wichita, Kansas
1st US parade with floats (Mardi Gras-Mobile, Alabama)
V-2/WAC-Corporal rocket 1st object to reach 5x the speed of sound at White Sands Missile Range in New Mexico
Steam shovel patented by William Otis, Philadelphia
PopeGregory XIIIannounces the New Style calendar, commonly known as the Gregorian calendar
The "Battle of Los Angeles" takes place, a series of anti-aircraft engagements over the city in response to a rumored but false Japanese attack. It would last until the morning of the following day.
Simplon railroad tunnel through the Alps in Switzerland achieves breakthrough after 7-1/2 years of construction
A special commission of the U.S. Congress releases a report that condemns the practice of Japanese internment during World War II.
US Supreme Court 1st rules a law unconstitutional (Marbury v Madison)
Corona reconnaissance satellite program, in existence from 1959 to 1972, is declassified
Puffin, publisher of author Roald Dhal, announces it will now print his books with their original unaltered text alongside those with offensive language removed, after widespread criticism and debate
Oldest Muslim graves in Europe, from the 8th century, identified at a burial site in Nimes, France

Panamanian boxing legendRoberto Duránwins 4th world title in different weight divisions when he beats Iran Barkley in a 12 round split decision in Atlantic City; 37-year-old Durán wins WBC middleweight title
Kevin Porter, NJ, sets NBA record with 29 assists in a game
Jim Kelly (Houston USFL) passes for pro football record 574 yds
LA Lakers centerKareem Abdul-Jabbarscores his 36,000th NBA point in a 97-93 win over the Suns in Phoenix
Boston Celtics begin an 18 NBA game winning streak
United Nations-backed COVAX initiative begins delivering vaccines to middle and low income countries with first AstraZeneca shipment to Ghana
Brian Ortegawins against Yair Rodríguez at UFC Fight Night 237, earning Performance of the Night
Siwinowe Kesibwi (Shawnee Sun) is the first Native American Indian language monthly magazine
Estonia declares independence from Russia
Peace treaty gives Estonia independence
WWII: US troops defeat Japanese forces in Manila, ending the military occupoation
1st transcontinental flight in 24 hrs flying time arrives in Florida
Frances Langford records "When You Wish Upon a Star"
Walter Piston's 5th Symphony, commissioned by the Juilliard School of Music for their 50th anniversary, premiere performance by the Juilliard Orchestra, conducted by Jean Morel
Du Pont begins commercial production of nylon toothbrush bristles
NASA Explorer S-45 mission; ionosphere beacon satellite fails to reach Earth orbit
Dutch schlager singer Heintje Simons (14) wins his 7th gold record
Winslow Homer, American painter (Gulfstream), born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 1910)
Judah Folkman, American pediatric surgeon and cancer researcher (angiogenesis), born in Cleveland, Ohio (d. 2008)
Hristo Prodanov, Bulgarian mountaineer, 13th person and 1st Bulgarian to climb Mt Everest who died on his descent, born in Bulgaria (d. 1984)
Ibn Battuta, Berber writer and the greatest Muslim traveller who went further than Marco Polo (Riḥlah), born in Tangier, Morocco (d. 368 or 377)
Colonel William Tate and his force of 1,000-1,500 soldiers surrender after the Last Invasion of Britain (according to legend, to Welsh women in tall black hats, mistaken for elite guards regiment)
Last sighting of Dutch trading ship De Hoop by its sister ship De Liefde near the Mariana islands, it's never seen again
Claudio Monteverdi's opera "L'Orfeo" premieres in Mantua, the oldest opera still regularly performed
George Rogers Clark captures Vincennes (Ind) from British
The signing of the Treaty of Yandaboo marks the end of the First Burmese War
King Louis-Philippe abdicates, 2nd French republic declared
